facts.text: Ogdensburg was founded in 1749 by French Jesuit missionary Abbé Francois Picquet, who built Fort de la Présentation to convert Native Americans to Christianity.
The city’s name honors Samuel Ogden, an early landowner who helped develop the area in the late 18th century.
Ogdensburg’s waterfront sits at the mouth of the Oswegatchie River on the south bank of the St. Lawrence River, making it New York’s northernmost port on the river.
The historic Fort de la Présentation site, now a museum, preserves the original 1749 French mission and offers guided tours of the preserved fort walls.

Railroad history runs through Ogdensburg, where the 1849 Ogdensburg & Lake Champlain Railroad and the 1853 Rome, Watertown & Ogdensburg Railroad linked the city to northern New York and southeastern Canada.
The Frederic Remington Art Museum, housed in a former armory, showcases works by the famed American artist who once lived in Ogdensburg.
